workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Give me the manager view on this Lucerne Publishing meeting transcript. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Lucerne Publishing
Date: 2024-01-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Kevin (Lucerne Publishing)

[00:02:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on whitespace analysis from our last session.

[00:05:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): On the whitespace analysis front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:08:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Kevin (Lucerne Publishing): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:18:00] Kevin (Lucerne Publishing): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-G trap (Invents action items from speculative discussion); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-G failure mode (Invents action items from speculative discussion)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-G -- Invents action items from speculative discussion
